frameworks.name: reference-dependent choice
frameworks.one_line: choice depends on variance in recent history of offers

frameworks.name: arousal-driven valuation
frameworks.one_line: pharmacologically reducing arousal alters reference-dependence in intertemporal choice

frameworks.name: temporal discounting
frameworks.one_line: tendency to prefer immediate over delayed rewards

frameworks.name: emotional-arousal account of impulsivity
frameworks.one_line: claim that immediate rewards are more arousing than delayed ones

core_question: Does pharmacologically reducing arousal change reference-dependence in intertemporal choice?
key_assumptions: arousal can be indexed by pupil dilation
reference-dependence reflects recent-history variance in offers
manipulations: variance structure of the more variable reward option (immediate vs delayed)
key_findings: propranolol reduces reference-dependence in intertemporal choices
choices under propranolol are less influenced by recent offer history
choices under propranolol are more internally consistent
interpretations: arousal causally contributes to reference-dependent intertemporal choice
context-dependence of valuation is at least partly arousal-mediated
